# **Overview** {#overview}

This document contains references and guidelines for AYO API Services. This API allows venue partners to integrate their system or application into AYO. The venue partner can validate the booking schedule for their courts that was booked in AYO.

# **Host** {#host}

Below is the host for an API:

| URL | Usage |
| :---- | :---- |
| https://api.ayo.co.id/api/v2/third-party/ | AYO API Production URL |
| [https://](https://api.ayo.co.id/)sandbox.ayodev.xyz/api/v2/third-party/ | AYO API Development URL |

# **Authentication** {#authentication}

In order to use the API, you will need a fulfillment service ID, such as a ***venue code***, an ***API token***, and a ***Venue secret key***. You must request access by contacting AYO and sending an email to [support@ayo.co.id.](mailto:support@ayo.co.id) Then we will provide you with access to the keys we have already mentioned.

* **Venue Code:** is a 10-character unique code for each venue registered with AYO which is automatically generated by the AYO system.  
* **API Token**: The API token/key is a unique code that functions to provide login access and connect code from one developer to another. As already explained, the API key is helpful in the authentication process when the API is executed.  
* **Venue Secret Key:** The secret key to generating signatures on each request.

* **Signature:** A mechanism to ensure that the data in each request and response cannot be hijacked by unauthorized users. This signature is generated using the SHA512-HMAC algorithm.

* **HMAC**: an acronym for Hash-based message authentication, which is a mechanism for calculating message authentication codes. It involves a cryptographic hash function combined with a secret key. Just like the name, HMAC's purpose is to authenticate messages.

# **Signature** {#signature}

To secure the data sent between the AYO system and external parties, we use a signature mechanism for every request. This is intended so that data sent through the AYO system to external parties or vice versa is properly maintained and prevents data manipulation by unauthorized parties. We use HMAC with SHA512 as the algorithm to generate the request signature. Here is the step on how we generate the signature:

**Example Request Body:**

1. **Sorting the data by key in ascending order:**

2. **Convert the sorted data to Query String:**

3. **Prepare the Private/Secret Key:**

4. **Generating the Signature (PHP):**

5. **Signature result:**

6. **To verify the generated signature is correct, please check it on an online signature generator such as [https://hmacgenerator.com/](https://hmacgenerator.com/) or a similar tool.**

![][image2]

***Generate signature with https://hmacgenerator.com***

# **Request Headers** {#request-headers}

Please send mandatory headers for every request as in the example below:

| Key | Value | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Accept | application/json | Accepting requests as the JSON |
| Content-Type | application/json | Content request as the JSON |

# **API Resources** {#api-resources}

1. ## **Get Bookings** {#get-bookings}

This endpoint retrieves bookings from the AYO platform. This endpoint will retrieve bookings created by the AYO users that have the status **SUCCESS**, **FINISHED**, **PENDING**, and **CANCELLED**. There are some optional parameters that you can provide at the request. Here is the list of request parameters:

### **Body Request (Payload):**

| Field | Is Mandatory | Description |
| :---- | :---: | ----- |
| token | Yes | API token |
| signature | Yes | Hash HMAC with SHA512 |
| booking\_id | No | This will be automatically generated from the AYO system. |
| field\_name | No | The field name must be the same as the field name on AYO. |
| date | No | Booking date with a format YYYY-MM-DD |
| start\_time | No | Booking start time with format HH:MM:SS |
| end\_time | No | Booking end time with format HH:MM:SS |

| booking\_source | No | There are 2 types of booking sources: “*orders*” and “*reservation*”, please use one of them if you want to filter by the booking source. |
| :---- | :---: | :---- |
| start\_date | No | Optional filter by date range (start date) |
| end\_date | No | Optional filter by date range (end date) |

**Request Method**:	**GET**

**API Endpoint**:	**API-BASE-URL**/list-bookings/:**venue\_code**

**Example Request:**

**Example Response:**

Below are some examples if you need filtering by some params:

2. ## **Notify Booking Information** {#notify-booking-information}

This endpoint will be used for the AYO platform to send new booking information or change notification messages to the venue's third-party platforms. A third-party API endpoint is **required** to inform us what the target URL or API endpoint is to receive the booking data that was sent by us. Below are examples of requests and responses:

**Request Method**:	**POST**

**API Endpoint**:	**THIRD-PARTY-API-BASE-URL**

**Body Request (Payload) will be sent to the Venue's third party:**

| Field | Description |
| :---- | ----- |
| order\_detail\_id | An order Id from the AYO system. |
| booking\_id | A unique booking ID was automatically created by the AYO system. |
| field\_id | A field Id that refers to the venue field name. |
| field\_name | The venue’s field name that stored in the AYO system. |
| date | Booking date with format YYYY-MM-DD |
| start\_time | Booking start time with format HH:MM:SS |
| end\_time | Booking end time with format HH:MM:SS |

| total\_price | The total price booking order transaction. |
| :---- | :---- |
| status | Booking status. |
| booker\_name | The user name who’s booking. |
| booker\_phone | Phone number of the user. |
| booker\_email | Email address of the user. |
| booking\_source | This field will determine where the booking was created (*AYO app or AVM services by the Venue owner*). |
| created\_at | A timestamp (date and time) when the booking was created. |

Below here is the example request and the expected response:

## **Notify Booking Information Body Request parameter:**

**Example Request:**

**Expected Response from Third-Party Endpoint:**

3. **Create Reservation (*Send Booking Information*)**

This endpoint will be used for sending booking information from a third-party system to the AYO platform. Here is the list of the request parameters that you should attach to the body request:

### **Request Method**:	**POST**

**API Endpoint**:	**API-BASE-URL**/create-reservation

### **Body Request (Payload)**

| Field | Mandatory | Format | Description |
| :---- | :---: | :---: | ----- |
| token | Yes | \- | API token |
| signature | Yes | HMAC | Hash HMAC with SHA512 |
| booking\_id | No | \- | This will be automatically generated from the AYO system. |
| field\_id | Yes | INTEGER | Please take the field\_id value from API endpoint venue field list in section E. |
| date | Yes | YYYY-MM-DD | Booking date. |
| start\_time | Yes | HH:MM:SS | Booking start time. |
| end\_time | Yes | HH:MM:SS | Booking end time. |

| total\_price | Yes | INTEGER | Booking price. |
| :---- | :---: | ----- | :---- |
| user.name | No |  | Optional param, if the booking type is a reservation, please send the complete user data (*name, email, and phone*) |
| user.email | No | Valid Email | Optional |
| user.phone | No | Valid Phone Number | Optional |

**Example Request (User Reservation):**

**Example Request (Internal Reservation):**

**Example Response:**

4. ## **Cancel Reservation** {#cancel-reservation}

This endpoint will be used to cancel existing reservations in AYO. Just information, that only the reservation (booking\_source is “reservation”) can be canceled by API. To cancel a reservation with the booking source “order” only, it can be done by AVM.

### **Request Method**:	**POST**

**API Endpoint**:	**API-BASE-URL**/cancel-reservation

### **Body Request (Payload)**

| Field | Mandatory | Format | Description |
| :---- | :---: | :---: | ----- |
| token | Yes | \- | API token |
| signature | Yes | HMAC | Hash HMAC with SHA512 |
| order\_detail\_id | Yes | \- | Order detail id with booking source is “reservation”. |

**Example Request (User Reservation):**

**Example Response:**

5. ## **Get Venue Fields** {#get-venue-fields}

This endpoint will be used for showing existing active fields in the AYO System.

### **Request Method**:	**GET**

**API Endpoint**:	**API-BASE-URL**/list-fields

**Example Request:**

**Example Response:**

# **HTTP Status Codes** {#http-status-codes}

Here is the list of codes.

| HTTP Code | Error Message | Description |
| :---: | :---- | :---- |
| **401** | Unauthenticated: | The request is not attached token, signature, or private key. |
| **401** | Invalid Signature | The signature value is not valid for the body request. |
| **401** | Unauthenticated: Invalid token | The token is not valid, or the AYO system cannot verify the token. |
| **200** | Success | The request was successful. |
| **500** | Server Error | There was an error on the server during the processing of the request |
